title: Festuca rigidoaspera
taxonomy: Festuca rigidoaspera is classified as a species of grass in the genus Festuca. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. The genus Festuca is a group of grasses that are typically perennial and native to the Northern Hemisphere. Festuca rigidoaspera is further classified as a member of the subgenus Festuca within the genus. The full classification of Festuca rigidoaspera is therefore Poaceae > Festuca > subgenus Festuca.

intro: Festuca rigidoaspera, also known as hard fescue or Korean fescue, is a species of grass native to East Asia that has been traditionally used for its medicinal and edible properties. The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Festuca rigidoaspera include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Festuca rigidoaspera is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in the form of dried leaves and roots in various herbal preparations, such as infusions and poultices.
